verdict: RIVAL leads by ~8.8 xPts — captain and Mbeumo are identical so the gap is purely roster depth: rival fields more attacking returners and two real sub-8% differentials (Mateta, Rogers) while user runs a thinner XI with only one differential.
key_differentiators: Captaincy is a wash: both captain Haaland (EV 16.41).
The 8.8-pt gap is entirely XI depth: rival's full 3-4-3 outscores user's XI.
Differential leverage: rival holds two sub-8% punts (Rogers 4.1%, Mateta 6.8%); user holds only Mbeumo (14.2%).
Squad hygiene: user correctly removes doubtful falling Gabriel; rival keeps him as dead bench weight.
executive_summary: GW38: both managers correctly captain Haaland with Salah VC — armband is a draw. User's brief is tactically immaculate on the margin (Gabriel-OUT/Mbeumo-IN + chip-hold are textbook) but trails 62.5 to 71.3 on raw projection. The gap is roster depth: rival's full 3-4-3 plus two sub-8% differentials (Rogers, Mateta) carry rank-up upside the user's lone Mbeumo cannot match. Highest-leverage action: upgrade weakest XI slot toward a starting attacking returner or add a true sub-8% differential (Rogers) to close the depth gap.
